name: kmerdecon

metadata.name: KmerDecon
metadata.summary: A fast, memory-efficient tool for decontaminating sequencing reads using Bloom filters.

documentation:
[![PyPI version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/KmerDecon.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/KmerDecon/)
# KmerDecon

KmerDecon is a fast, memory-efficient tool for decontaminating sequencing reads using Bloom filters or Cuckoo filters. It generate detailed reports of contaminants in sequencing data.

## Authors
- Yujia Feng
- Xiaoyi Chen
- Yuxiang Li

## Installation

### Prerequisites:

- Python 3.6 or higher
- pip package manager

### Steps:

Run the following command inside the directory:
  ```
  pip install .
  ```

## Usage

### 1. Building the Bloom Filter or Cuckoo Filter

Generate a Bloom filter from contamination source sequences. Generate a Cuckoo filter, use `-s cuckoo`. Use `kbuild --help` for more detail.

```
kbuild -c contamination.fasta -s bloom -o contamination_filter.bf
```


**Optional Arguments:**

- `kmer-length`: Length of k-mers to generate (e.g., 31). If not provided, the tool determines the optimal k-mer length automatically.
- `expected-elements`: Expected number of unique k-mers. If not provided, it is estimated using HyperLogLog.
- `exclude-filter`: A .bf filter or .cms file path. If provided, any k-mers present in the excluded filter will not be encoded into the new build filter.
- `max-memory`: Maximum memory in GB for the Bloom filter. Adjusts parameters to fit within this limit.
- `false-positive-rate`: Desired false positive rate (default: 0.01).

if choose build Cuckoo filter:
- `capacity-of-cuckoofilter`: The capacity of cuckoo filter

### 2. Decontaminating Reads

Filter out contaminated reads from your sequencing data. Use `kdecon --help` for more detail.

Use bloom filter:
```
kdecon -i reads.fastq -d example_filter/hg38.bf -s bloom -o output
```
Use `-s cuckoo` for Cuckoo filter.

**Optional Arguments:**

- `threshold`: Fraction of matching k-mers to consider a read contaminated (default: 0.5).
- `kmer-length`: Length of k-mers used. If not provided, the k-mer length from the Bloom filter is used.
- `mode`: Operation mode, either filter (default) or states.
  - filter: Filters reads based on contamination levels.
  - states: Generates a states.csv report with contamination statistics. Columns:
	- {filter}_avgSimilarity: The average fraction of matching k-mers across all reads in that file for each filter.
	- {filter}_percentReadsPassing: The percentage of reads passing the threshold for each filter.

## Performance

### Highlights

- With default parameters, we achieves FPR = 0.002%, FNR = 0.05% on simulated human reads decontamination task.
- KmerDecon is memory efficient and uses 10 bits / kmer. (Popular too Kraken2 uses 32 bits / kmer)
- KmerDecon is fast and takse 5 min to filter 1 million reads of 150bp each (kraken2 takes ~8min, both on single thread)
- Multi-threads parallel building supported.

### Full Reports
- To read the full performance report, please see: [Here](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1shEp8LZAC5w_qR0p8BzsjBeAsOffvDIH/view?usp=sharing)
- To recreate the results on the report, please see: [Here](reproducibility.md)


## Dependencies

- `bitarray>=2.1.0`
- `biopython>=1.78`
- `mmh3>=2.5.1`
- `hyperloglog>=0.0.12`

Install dependencies manually with:

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
```
## Referenced Code
 The python module of cuckoofilter is adapted from:

 Author: Huy Do

 Repository: https://github.com/huydhn/cuckoo-filter/blob/master/cuckoo/filter.py
 
 License: MIT

## Contributing

Contributions and PRs are welcome!

## License

This project is licensed under the MIT License. See the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.

## Contact

For questions or suggestions, please open an issue.

modules.name: KmerDecon.utils
documentation: src/KmerDecon/utils.py

functions.name: KmerDecon.utils.generate_kmers
documentation:
Generate k-mers from a given sequence.

Args:
    sequence (str): The nucleotide sequence.
    k (int): Length of k-mers to generate.

Yields:
    Iterator[str]: A generator of k-mers.

modules.name: KmerDecon
documentation: KmerDecon: A package for fast, memory-efficient decontamination of sequencing reads using Bloom filters.
src/KmerDecon/__init__.py

classes.name: KmerDecon.bloom_filter.BloomFilter

methods.name: KmerDecon.bloom_filter.BloomFilter.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Bloom filter.

Args:
    expected_elements (int): Estimated number of elements to store.
    false_positive_rate (float): Desired false positive rate.
    kmer_length (int): Length of k-mers used.

methods.name: KmerDecon.bloom_filter.BloomFilter._get_size
documentation:
Calculate the size of the bit array (m) given expected elements (n) and false positive rate (p).

Returns:
    int: Size of the bit array.

methods.name: KmerDecon.bloom_filter.BloomFilter._get_hash_count
documentation:
Calculate the optimal number of hash functions (k) given bit array size (m) and expected elements (n).

Returns:
    int: Number of hash functions.

methods.name: KmerDecon.bloom_filter.BloomFilter.add
documentation:
Add an item to the Bloom filter using 64-bit hashes.

Args:
    item (Any): The item to add.

methods.name: KmerDecon.bloom_filter.BloomFilter.__contains__
documentation:
Check if an item is in the Bloom filter.

Args:
    item (Any): The item to check.

Returns:
    bool: True if the item is probably in the filter, False if definitely not.

methods.name: KmerDecon.bloom_filter.BloomFilter.save
documentation:
Save the Bloom filter to a compressed file using gzip.

Args:
    filename (str): The filename to save the filter to.

methods.name: KmerDecon.bloom_filter.BloomFilter.combine
documentation:
Combines the input Bloom Filter. Only used for parallel process.

Args:
    bf (BloomFilter): The Bloom Filter to combine with.

methods.name: KmerDecon.bloom_filter.BloomFilter.load
documentation:
Load a Bloom filter from a compressed file using gzip.

Args:
    filename (str): The filename to load the filter from.

Returns:
    BloomFilter: The loaded Bloom filter.
A Bloom filter for efficient k-mer storage and lookup.

modules.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter
documentation: The following code is adapted from:
Repository: https://github.com/huydhn/cuckoo-filter/blob/master/cuckoo/filter.py
License: MIT

classes.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.CuckooTemplate

methods.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.CuckooTemplate.__init__
documentation:
Initialize Cuckoo filter parameters.

capacity: The size of the filter, it defines how many buckets the
    filter contains.

error_rate: The desired error rate, the lower the error rate and the
    bigger the bucket size, the longer the fingerprint needs to be.

bucket_size : The maximum number of fingerprints a bucket can hold.
    The default size is 4, which closely approaches the best size for
    FPP between 0.00001 and 0.002 (see Fan et al.).  Also according to
    the author, if your targeted FPP is greater than 0.002, a bucket
    size of 2 is more space efficient.

max_kicks : The number of times entries are kicked / moved around
    before the filter is considered full.  Defaults to 500 used by
    Fan et al. in the cited paper.

methods.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.CuckooTemplate.index
documentation: Calculate the (first) index of an item in the filter.

methods.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.CuckooTemplate.indices
documentation:
Calculate all possible indices for the item.  The fingerprint must be a
bit array.

methods.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.CuckooTemplate.fingerprint
documentation:
Take an item and returns its fingerprint in bits.  The fingerprint of
an item is computed by truncating its Murmur hashing (murmur3) to the
fingerprint size.

Return a bit array representation of the fingerprint.

methods.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.CuckooTemplate.load_factor
documentation: Provide some useful details about the current state of the filter.

The base template of a Cuckoo filter.  Do not use this
directly.

classes.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.BCuckooFilter

methods.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.BCuckooFilter.__init__
documentation:
Initialize Cuckoo filter parameters.

capacity: The size of the filter, it defines how many buckets the
    filter contains.

error_rate: The desired error rate, the lower the error rate and the
    bigger the bucket size, the longer the fingerprint needs to be.

bucket_size : The maximum number of fingerprints a bucket can hold.
    The default size is 4, which closely approaches the best size for
    FPP between 0.00001 and 0.002 (see Fan et al.).  Also according
    to the author, if your targeted FPP is greater than 0.002, a
    bucket size of 2 is more space efficient.

max_kicks : The number of times entries are kicked / moved around
    before the filter is considered full.  Defaults to 500 used by
    Fan et al. in the above paper.

methods.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.BCuckooFilter._bit_index
documentation: Convert a bucket index to its location, bit index, in the bit array.

methods.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.BCuckooFilter._find_and_replace
documentation:
Find an exact fingerprint the specified bucket and replace it with
another fingerprint.  Return False if there is no such fingerprint.

methods.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.BCuckooFilter._include
documentation: Check if a fingerprint exists in the bucket at the specified index.

methods.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.BCuckooFilter._insert
documentation:
Insert a fingerprint into the bucket at the specified index. Basically,
it set the corresponding bits in the bucket:

        Bucket               Bucket
------------------------------------------ ...
| F1 | F2 | F3 | F4 || F1 | F2 | F3 | F4 |

When the bucket is full (all bits are set), the function will return
False.

methods.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.BCuckooFilter._swap
documentation:
Swap a fingerprint with a random fingerprint of the bucket at the
specified index.

methods.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.BCuckooFilter.insert
documentation:
Insert an into the filter, throw an exception if the filter is full and
the insertion fails.

methods.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.BCuckooFilter.contains
documentation: Check if an item is in the filter, return false if it does not exist.

methods.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.BCuckooFilter.delete
documentation: Remove an item from the filter, return false if it does not exist.

methods.name: KmerDecon.cuckoofilter.BCuckooFilter.save
documentation:
Save the Cuckoo filter to a compressed file using gzip.

Args:
    filename (str): The filename to save the filter to.

Implement a compact Cuckoo filter using bit array so that it can keep
millions of items.

modules.name: KmerDecon.build_data_structure
documentation: src/KmerDecon/build_bloom_filter.py

functions.name: KmerDecon.build_data_structure.init_hll_worker
documentation:
Initializes the HyperLogLog worker for parallel proccessing.
Each worker gets its own copy of the exclude filter.

functions.name: KmerDecon.build_data_structure.init_bf_worker
documentation:
Initializes the Bloom filter worker for parallel proccessing.
Each worker gets its own copy of the exclude filter.
Each worker creates its own Bloom filter.

functions.name: KmerDecon.build_data_structure.process_seq_for_kmers
documentation:
This is only used when using multiple cores.
Estimate the number of unique k-mers in the sequence using HyperLogLog.

Args:
    seq SeqRecord: Sequence to calculate for.
    k (int): Length of k-mers.

Returns:
    int: The number of kmers in the sequence.
    HyperLogLog: The HyperLogLog generated from this sequence.

functions.name: KmerDecon.build_data_structure.estimate_unique_kmers
documentation:
Estimate the number of unique k-mers in the contamination sequences using HyperLogLog.

Args:
    contamination_fasta (str): Path to the contamination FASTA file.
    k (int): Length of k-mers.
    exclude_filter (BloomFilter): excluded filter used.

Returns:
    int: Estimated number of unique k-mers.

functions.name: KmerDecon.build_data_structure.estimate_unique_kmers_parallel
documentation:
Estimate the number of unique k-mers in the contamination sequences using HyperLogLog with multiprocessing.

Args:
    records List[SeqRecord]: The list of sequences.
    k (int): Length of k-mers.
    exclude_filter (BloomFilter): excluded filter used.

Returns:
    int: Estimated number of unique k-mers.

functions.name: KmerDecon.build_data_structure.process_sequence_chunk
documentation:
Used in parallel processing.
Process each sequence record to add k-mers to the Bloom filter.

Args:
    chunk (List[SeqRecord]): The list of sequences to build the Bloom filter for.
    k (int): Length of k-mers.

Returns:
    total_kmers (int): Total kmers in the input chunk.
    bloom_filter (BloomFilter): The Bloom filter constructed by the worker.
    total_seq (int): The number of sequences.
    kmers_added (int): The number of kmers not excluded by the exclude filter.
